notes: Starts up even faster and lighter. At launch, Yahoo KeyKey 2 now reads its dictionary in a single pass instead of two, and it builds the 速成 (Simplex) code table and the 反查／拆碼提示 reverse-lookup index only when you actually use them. If you type only 倉頡 or 拼音, that's less work and less memory every time the app starts. Nothing you type changes. · 拼音 stays responsive on unusual input. Typing a long run of letters that can't form valid syllables no longer makes the pinyin composer do more and more work on every keystroke.

Faster startup and lower memory use. At launch, Yahoo KeyKey 2 no longer builds a large in-memory copy of its whole dictionary just to work out which characters are most common — it now reads only what it needs. The app starts up quicker and uses less memory, and nothing you type changes. · 拼音 typing is a little more efficient. While you're composing pinyin, the engine skips repeated sorting work it used to do on every keystroke. You'll notice it most with long phrases. The candidates you see, and their order, are exactly the same as before.

Removed 候選字大小 from the input menu. The coarse 小／中／大 shortcuts in the input-method menu-bar item are gone — candidate text size is now set with the fine-grained slider under 設定… ▸ 一般, which lets you pick any size in the supported range instead of just three fixed steps. Your current size is unchanged.

A bug-fix release for the new 拼音 (Pinyin) input method. · 拼音: number keys now pick a candidate directly. Pressing 1–9 selects that candidate — a single syllable commits right away (like 倉頡／速成), and in a phrase it picks that syllable and moves to the next. Space still commits the whole phrase at once. · 拼音: the 反查／拆碼提示 hint now shows pinyin. With the code hint on, candidates in 拼音 show their pinyin reading (e.g. `wo`, `ni hao`) instead of the 倉頡 code — for both the syllables you're typing and the 聯想 suggestions that follow. · Fixed: the in-app What's New now shows the current version. The 設定… ▸ What's New pane was stuck on an older release's notes; it now reflects the version you're running.

New input method: 拼音 (Pinyin). Alongside 倉頡 and 速成, Yahoo KeyKey 2 now offers a 拼音 phrase input method that composes whole phrases (no tone marks needed). Space／Return commit the phrase; 1–9 pick a candidate for the syllable at the cursor; ←／→ move between syllables to correct them; Backspace deletes and Esc clears; use an apostrophe (') to split ambiguous syllables (e.g. xi'an → 西安).

Fixed: the candidate text size slider now updates as you drag. In 設定… ▸ 一般, dragging the 候選字大小 (candidate text size) slider used to leave the shown value and the handle stuck even though the size did change. The slider and its label now track your dragging live.

Clearer "already up to date" message. When you check for updates and you're already on the latest version, the confirmation dialog now reads more naturally. · About now shows the build time. The version line in About now reads like v2.4.1 (24) · 2026-Jul-06 13:34:56 UTC, adding the exact UTC build time alongside the version and build number.

Two new KeyKey features plus a settings fix. · New: 反查／拆碼提示 (Cangjie code hint) — turn on 反查提示 to show each character's 倉頡 code beside the candidate (e.g. 倉 → 人口竹口); handy in 速成 and 聯想. Off by default. · New: 臨時英數 (quick English) — hold Shift and press a letter to type that English letter directly, like the original Yahoo! KeyKey; case follows Caps Lock, and everything else stays Chinese. · Fixed: the 倉頡版本 (五代／三代) choice in Settings now saves correctly instead of reverting.
